name: Joe Pass
abstract: Joe Pass (born Joseph Anthony Jacobi Passalacqua; January 13, 1929 – May 23, 1994) was an American jazz guitarist. Pass recorded and performed live with pianist Oscar Peterson, composer Duke Ellington, and vocalist Ella Fitzgerald, and he is generally esteemed as one of the most notable jazz guitarists of the 20th century for his solo gu…

about_quotes.speaker: Herb Ellis
about_quotes.evidence: Tell us about some of your duo work with Joe Pass. ... I loved him and he was my favorite guitarist and still is. And we had a way of playing. We could play, we knew how to back each other up
about_quotes.doc_title: Herb Ellis interviewed by Michael Woods, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: James Chirillo
about_quotes.evidence: Joe Pass and Grant Green, guys like that, of course Wes. But I hadn't even heard of Charlie Parker when I went down there
about_quotes.doc_title: James Chirillo interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: I was working at the China Trader with Bobby Troop, but I could always leave and come back, and had pretty good subs, like, you know, Joe Pass, John Gray, Al Viola.

about_quotes.speaker: John Heard
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah I recorded with him. I just worked with him not long ago. He has a great ... there is a nice album I recorded with Joe.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Heard interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Toots Thielemans
about_quotes.evidence: one important appearance was with Oscar Peterson. Of course, with Louie Bellson, Joe Pass, and me, and Oscar, of course.
about_quotes.doc_title: Toots Thielemans — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: Joe Pass is on guitar and Ray Brown on bass and Duke. Just the four of us.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
